projects.title: Telegram Automation System
projects.summary: This project aims to develop a robust automation system for Telegram, enabling users to define and execute custom actions based on specific triggers. The system will feature a user-friendly interface for rule management, secure data storage, and reliable background processing to automate various Telegram interactions.

plan.title: Telegram Automation System
plan.summary: This project aims to develop a robust automation system for Telegram, enabling users to define and execute custom actions based on specific triggers. The system will feature a user-friendly interface for rule management, secure data storage, and reliable background processing to automate various Telegram interactions.

roadmap.description: Establish the project environment, implement basic Telegram bot functionality, set up user authentication, and design the initial database schema for storing users and automation rules.

Develop the core automation logic, including abstracting triggers and actions. Implement mechanisms for users to define, store, and manage their automation rules through the bot interface. Integrate background processing for rule execution.

Enhance the system with more complex triggers and actions, robust error handling, comprehensive logging, and thorough testing. Prepare the application for production deployment, including containerization and cloud infrastructure setup.

milestones.title: Basic Bot Operational
milestones.criteria: The Telegram bot successfully responds to `/start` and `/help` commands, user registration/login is functional, and the core database schema is defined and implemented.

milestones.title: First Automation Rule Executed
milestones.criteria: A user can define a simple automation rule (e.g., 'if message contains X, reply with Y'), and the system successfully detects the trigger and executes the corresponding action.

milestones.title: Rule Management UI Complete
milestones.criteria: Users can fully manage their automation rules (list, create, edit, delete) using intuitive bot commands or an inline keyboard interface.

milestones.title: Production Deployment Ready
milestones.criteria: The application is containerized with Docker, deployed to a cloud environment (e.g., Heroku/AWS), and includes basic monitoring and logging for stability.

tasks: Initialize Git repository and set up Python virtual environment.
Register a new Telegram bot via BotFather and obtain API token.
Configure basic logging and error handling for the bot.
Define initial project structure and add a `requirements.txt`.
Design the database schema for Users, Rules, Triggers, and Actions.
Integrate an ORM (e.g., SQLAlchemy) with SQLite for initial development.
Implement user registration and authentication flow via bot commands.
Develop functions for storing and retrieving user data from the database.
Define abstract classes or interfaces for Triggers and Actions.
Implement the main event listener to process incoming Telegram updates.
Develop the core logic to match incoming updates against defined triggers.
Begin implementing a simple concrete trigger (e.g., `on_text_match`).
Develop functionality to save and load user-defined rules to/from the database.
Integrate the rule execution engine with the event listener.
Test the full lifecycle of a basic automation rule: define, save, trigger, execute.

resources.name: Telegram Bot API Documentation
resources.description: The official reference for all Telegram bot capabilities and API methods.

resources.name: python-telegram-bot library
resources.description: A popular, feature-rich Python wrapper for the Telegram Bot API, simplifying bot development.

resources.name: SQLAlchemy ORM Documentation
resources.description: A powerful SQL toolkit and Object Relational Mapper for Python, crucial for database interaction.

resources.name: Docker Documentation
resources.description: Essential for containerizing the application, ensuring consistent environments and simplified deployment.

resources.name: Heroku Dev Center / AWS Free Tier Docs
resources.description: Guides for deploying Python applications to a cloud platform, useful for production hosting.

resources.name: APScheduler (Advanced Python Scheduler)
resources.description: Useful for implementing time-based triggers or scheduled tasks within the automation system.

resources.name: Redis Documentation
resources.description: A fast in-memory data store, useful for caching, rate-limiting, or managing task queues for scalability.

projectStructure.description: The system will follow a modular, event-driven architecture. Bot interactions are handled separately from the core automation logic and data persistence. Incoming Telegram updates drive the system, which then matches them against user-defined rules to execute actions.

folders.purpose: Contains all primary source code for the application.
Handles Telegram bot update processing, command routing, and user interaction logic.
Implements the main automation engine, including trigger detection, action execution, and rule processing.
Manages database connection, ORM setup, and migration scripts.
Defines database models for users, rules, triggers, actions, and other entities.
Stores configuration files, environment variable loading, and settings.
Contains common utility functions, helpers, and shared modules.
Houses unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for the application components.
Project documentation, API specifications, and user guides.
Contains Dockerfile and related containerization configurations for deployment.

messages.content: I've created a complete plan for "Telegram Automation System". Ask me anything about the roadmap, milestones, or next steps!

أكيد! ممكن نقسم "المرحلة الأولى" (Phase 1) لمرحلتين فرعيتين عشان تكون أكثر تنظيمًا وسهولة في التنفيذ:

---

**المرحلة 1.1: التخطيط والإعداد الأساسي (Planning & Basic Setup)**

*   **تحديد الأهداف الأولية:** ما هي أول وظيفة بسيطة تريد أن يؤديها البوت؟ (مثال: الرد على رسالة معينة، إرسال رسالة ترحيب).
*   **اختيار التقنية الأساسية:** تحديد لغة البرمجة (مثل Python أو Node.js) والمكتبة المناسبة للتعامل مع Telegram API (مثل `python-telegram-bot` لـ Python أو `telegraf.js` لـ Node.js).
*   **إعداد بيئة التطوير:** تثبيت الأدوات اللازمة (IDE، بيئة افتراضية).
*   **الحصول على Telegram Bot Token:** إنشاء البوت الخاص بك من BotFather والحصول على الرمز المميز.

---

**المرحلة 1.2: الاتصال الأساسي والتفاعل الأول (Core Connection & First Interaction)**

*   **كتابة الكود للاتصال بـ Telegram API:** استخدام المكتبة المختارة لإنشاء اتصال أساسي مع بوتك.
*   **تنفيذ أمر بسيط (مثل `/start`):** جعل البوت يستجيب لأول أمر يتلقاه.
*   **إرسال رسالة "أهلاً وسهلاً":** برمجة البوت لإرسال رسالة ترحيب بسيطة.
*   **استقبال رسالة نصية بسيطة:** التأكد من أن البوت يمكنه استقبال الرسائل من المستخدمين.

---

هذا التقسيم بيخليك تركز على خطوة بخطوة، وبتبني أساس قوي قبل ما تنتقل للميزات الأكثر تعقيدًا. ايه رأيك؟

projects.title: Personal Portfolio Website
projects.summary: A modern, fast, and SEO-optimized personal portfolio website designed to showcase projects, blog posts, and contact information. Built with Next.js, Tailwind CSS, and a PostgreSQL database for dynamic content management.

plan.title: Personal Portfolio Website
plan.summary: A modern, fast, and SEO-optimized personal portfolio website designed to showcase projects, blog posts, and contact information. Built with Next.js, Tailwind CSS, and a PostgreSQL database for dynamic content management.

roadmap.name: Design & Setup
roadmap.description: Initialize the project repository, configure the development environment, and design the UI/UX wireframes.

roadmap.name: Core Development
roadmap.description: Build the core frontend components, static pages, and implement responsive styling.

roadmap.name: Database & CMS Integration
roadmap.description: Set up the database, configure Prisma ORM, and build API routes for dynamic content.

roadmap.name: Deployment & Optimization
roadmap.description: Optimize performance, configure SEO metadata, and deploy the live site.

items.title: Project Initialization
items.description: Set up Next.js with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and ESLint.

items.title: UI/UX Wireframing
items.description: Design the layout for Home, Projects, and Contact pages.

items.title: Component Development
items.description: Build reusable UI components like Navbar, Footer, ProjectCard, and Button.

items.title: Page Implementation
items.description: Create static pages for Home, About, and Projects list.

items.title: Database Setup
items.description: Configure PostgreSQL database schema using Prisma ORM.

items.title: API Route Creation
items.description: Develop backend API endpoints to fetch projects and handle contact form submissions.

items.title: SEO & Performance Optimization
items.description: Configure metadata, open graph tags, and optimize image loading.

items.title: Deployment & Domain Configuration
items.description: Deploy the application to Vercel and link a custom domain.

database.description: PostgreSQL database managed via Prisma ORM to store dynamic portfolio content and contact inquiries.

folderStructure.description: Standard Next.js App Router structure optimized for scalability and clean separation of concerns.

folders.purpose: Contains pages, layouts, and API routes (App Router).
Utility functions, database clients (Prisma), and third-party integrations.
Global stylesheets and Tailwind CSS configurations.
Static assets such as images, icons, and fonts.

steps: Push the codebase to a GitHub repository.
Import the repository into the Vercel Dashboard.
Click Deploy to initiate the build and deployment process.
Configure custom domain settings in Vercel.
deployment.description: Serverless deployment platform optimized for Next.js applications, offering automatic SSL, global CDN, and preview deployments.

bestPractices.title: Responsive Design
bestPractices.description: Ensure the UI is fully responsive across mobile, tablet, and desktop screens.

bestPractices.title: Semantic HTML
bestPractices.description: Use proper semantic elements to improve accessibility and SEO.

bestPractices.title: Image Optimization
bestPractices.description: Utilize Next.js Image component for automatic format and size optimization.

bestPractices.title: Environment Variables
bestPractices.description: Secure sensitive credentials like database URLs using environment variables.

bestPractices.title: Component Reusability
bestPractices.description: Keep components modular and reusable to maintain a clean codebase.

bestPractices.title: Type Safety
bestPractices.description: Leverage TypeScript strictly to prevent runtime errors and improve developer experience.
